questions.question: Choose the correct word: '___ going to the party tonight.'
questions.explanation: They're = they are going → makes sense

questions.question: 'The box of apples ___ on the table.'
questions.explanation: Subject = 'box' (singular) → is. 'of apples' just describes the box

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I went to the store and I buy milk.
I went to the store and I bought milk.
I go to the store and I bought milk.
I go to the store and I will buy milk.
questions.explanation: 'went' = past tense, so 'bought' must also be past (consistency)

questions.question: 'This is ___ house, isn't it?'
questions.explanation: 'your' = belonging to you. 'you're house' = 'you are house' (wrong!)

questions.question: 'She put the keys ___ on the table.'
'It was ___ cold ___ go outside.'
questions.explanation: 'too cold' = excessively cold. 'to go' = infinitive verb form
